ppa_notes: Store-and-forward: 256x8 byte buffer (mem[0:255]) inferred as RAM is the dominant area structure; localparam MSG_CAP=256, MAX_MSG default 192 <= MSG_CAP. Single-clock byte-serial datapath, one byte per egress beat, so no wide combinational fan-in. Likely critical path is the egress mux (header byte composition vs mem[rd_ptr] read) plus m_ready qualification; it is shallow and single-cycle as the contract requires. Highest-toggle nodes are rd_ptr/wr_ptr byte pointers, pay_left/bytes_remaining counters, and the egress data mux. No pipeline added (simple leaf peripheral); single-cycle APB response with pready tied high per contract. Explicit register write enables via FSM-gated NBAs let synthesis infer enables; no manual clock gating. Length/oversize compare is a 16-bit magnitude comparator against csr_max_msg. CSR read path is a flat address-decoded mux (combinational prdata). No oversize buffering risk: oversize is flagged on the beat that would exceed MAX_MSG, so wr_ptr cannot exceed MSG_CAP. Tradeoff: store-and-forward adds intake latency and a full-message buffer but is the simplest structure that guarantees CONTRACT_TX_ERR no-partial-emission; a cut-through design was rejected because it cannot retract a started packet on a late underrun/oversize.
domain_crossing_notes: Single clock domain clk; no CDC crossings (domain_intent.yaml clock_domains/cdc_crossings empty). RESET is now correctly async-assert / SYNC-deassert per CONTRACT_TX_RESET: the external pin rst_n feeds ONLY a 2-FF reset deassertion synchronizer (rst_meta -> rst_sync_n) via always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n); rst_sync_n is the single internal active-low reset that drives ALL functional sequential logic (the main FSM/CSR/datapath block now uses always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_sync_n) with !rst_sync_n as the reset condition). When rst_n drops, rst_sync_n asserts immediately (async assert). When rst_n rises, rst_sync_n deasserts only after the 2-FF pipeline fills, aligned to clk (synchronous deassert). [Static lint fix: previously rst_sync_n was built but unused and the FSM keyed off raw rst_n, so deassert was asynchronous and the sync-deassert guarantee was unmet; that gap is now closed and verilator no longer flags rst_sync_n UNUSEDSIGNAL.] RDC: no_known_rdc for v0 per domain_intent.yaml (single reset). oag_domain_crossing_check.py --ip-dir mctp_tx_assembler --rtl-file rtl/mctp_tx_assembler.v -> status pass, 0 issues, clk/rst_n detected, 3 async-event blocks. No coherent multi-bit CDC exists, so no synchronizer/Gray/handshake mitigation is required. CDC/RDC closure is NOT claimed here; release-grade closure requires static/formal evidence per oag-cdc-rdc rules.
checks_run: iverilog 12.0 (stable) + verilator 5.046. Re-verified after the scoped RESET-path fix (and prior 2 ERR-path fixes). Parse/elaborate: `cd mctp_tx_assembler && iverilog -g2012 -t null -Wall rtl/mctp_tx_assembler.v` -> exit 0, no errors/warnings. `iverilog -t null -Wall rtl/mctp_tx_assembler.v` (default, no -g2012) -> exit 0, no errors/warnings. Static lint: `verilator --lint-only -Wall rtl/mctp_tx_assembler.v` -> exit 0, 0 warnings (was 3: rst_sync_n UNUSEDSIGNAL fixed by wiring the synchronized reset into all functional logic; MSG_CAP_LEN UNUSEDPARAM removed; pwdata[31:16] UNUSEDSIGNAL waived with a scoped /* verilator lint_off UNUSEDSIGNAL */ around the frozen 32-bit pwdata port plus a one-line rationale, since the widest CSR field is 16 bits and the port width is fixed by the interface). OAG dialect/PPA screen: `python3 .claude/scripts/oag_ppa_check.py mctp_tx_assembler/rtl/mctp_tx_assembler.v --json` -> status=pass, dialect=oag_sv_lite_v1, fail=0 warn=0 issues=0. OAG domain crossing screen: `python3 .claude/scripts/oag_domain_crossing_check.py --ip-dir mctp_tx_assembler --rtl-file mctp_tx_assembler/rtl/mctp_tx_assembler.v --json` -> status=pass, 0 issues, rtl_files=1, clk + rst_n detected, 3 async-event blocks. Developer-only directed smoke sims (written to /tmp, NOT part of deliverable/evidence, then deleted; expected values hand-derived from behavior_model/cycle_rules, independent of DUT): RESET fix verification: async-assert (rst_n dropped between clock edges) holds reset; after deassert + synchronizer fill, post-reset CSR/state defaults read back correct (CTRL=0, SRC_EID=0x00, MTU=64, MAX_MSG=192, STATUS busy=0, SENT_CNT=0, DROP_CNT=0, ERR_FLAGS=0) and a 130B@MTU64 message still emits 3 packets -> RESET PASS (sync-deassert + defaults + regression). Prior ERR-path regressions still hold: (A) mid-intake independent abort -> ZERO egress, underrun flag, DROP++, 1 irq_err; (B) normal 100B after abort -> 2 packets (108 bytes), SENT_CNT=1; (C) 250B oversize (MAX_MSG=192) -> ZERO egress, oversize flag, exactly 1 irq_err, DROP total=2; (D) fresh 20B after swallowed tail -> 1 packet (24 bytes); ERR-PATH PASS. Happy-path: 130B@MTU64 -> 3 packets (64,64,2), headers/SOM/EOM/SEQ correct -> HAPPY PASS. These smoke runs are debug confidence only; closure-grade scoreboard evidence is owned by the TB/sim agents and lint+sim will both re-run.
summary: Single-file synthesizable RTL mctp_tx_assembler.v (545 lines) realizing all 9 locked CONTRACT_TX_* contracts against the frozen interface doc, with 2 SCOPED ERR-path fixes (CONTRACT_TX_ERR) and 1 SCOPED RESET-path fix (CONTRACT_TX_RESET). RESET FIX (static-lint gap): the 2-FF reset deassertion synchronizer (rst_meta -> rst_sync_n) was previously dead (rst_sync_n unused; the FSM keyed off raw rst_n, making deassert asynchronous and violating the sync-deassert requirement). Now rst_n feeds ONLY the synchronizer and rst_sync_n is the single internal active-low reset for ALL functional sequential logic (main block uses always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_sync_n)); reset asserts asynchronously and deasserts synchronously to clk. Post-reset observable state is unchanged (busy=0, seq=0, counters=0, CSR defaults), so the RESET scoreboard scenario stays green. Also removed dead localparam MSG_CAP_LEN and added a scoped verilator UNUSEDSIGNAL waiver on pwdata[31:16] (frozen 32-bit port; widest CSR field is 16b) so verilator --lint-only -Wall is now exit 0 with 0 warnings. Store-and-forward FSM (IDLE->INTAKE->VALIDATE->EMIT_HDR->EMIT_PAY->DONE, plus ST_DISCARD for rejected-stream quiesce) buffers the whole message into mem[0:255], validates zero-length / oversize / underrun BEFORE any egress byte, then fragments into ceil(len/MTU) packets (short final packet), composes the DSP0236 4-byte header (byte0=0x01, byte1=dest_eid, byte2=CSR SRC_EID, byte3={SOM,EOM,SEQ[1:0],TO,MSG_TAG[2:0]}) followed by order-preserved payload, and asserts SOM/EOM and 2-bit SEQ (0 then +1 mod 4). FIX 1 (underrun / Bug 1): s_abort is now sampled as an INDEPENDENT sideband in ST_INTAKE (not gated by the s_valid/s_ready handshake) per doc; an abort sets ERR_FLAGS.underrun, DROP_CNT++, pulses irq_err, drops the buffer, and re-arms intake immediately (no s_last expected for an aborted message). FIX 2 (oversize tail-escape / Bug 2): after a mid-stream oversize reject the FSM enters ST_DISCARD and keeps consuming+discarding the offending stream's remaining bytes (s_ready held high, no buffering, no emission, no re-flag/re-count) until s_last, then re-arms; this closes the tail-escape that previously let bytes 193+ be mistaken for a new message and emitted (no partial message is emitted). The first-beat oversize path (MAX_MSG=0 edge) also routes to ST_DISCARD for consistency. s_ready stays low for a new message until the cycle after the EOM packet's m_eop (ST_DONE->ST_IDLE), giving CONTRACT_TX_SINGLEMSG. Egress holds m_valid/m_data while m_ready=0 (no byte lost). CSR map matches the frozen doc exactly (CTRL/SRC_EID/MTU/MAX_MSG/STATUS/IRQ_EN/ERR_FLAGS/SENT_CNT/DROP_CNT) with W1C on STATUS done/err and ERR_FLAGS, pready tied high, reset defaults SRC_EID=0x00, MTU=0x40, MAX_MSG=0x00C0. irq_done/irq_err are gated single-cycle pulses; SENT_CNT++ on EOM emission, DROP_CNT++ on each dropped message. Reset is async-assert/sync-deassert active-low per CONTRACT_TX_RESET. The 10 previously-passing behaviors are verified intact (happy-path + backpressure smoke regressions pass). No ontology/spec truth was modified; only rtl/ was written. Closure, lint signoff, coverage, and final completion are NOT claimed (may_claim_complete=false) and remain owned by the lint/sim/gate agents.
